pattern_recognition: Tree Traversal, Binary Search Tree Properties - We recognize this problem as a tree traversal problem where we need to validate the properties of a binary search tree (BST). The key signals are the in-order traversal of the tree yielding a sorted sequence of values.

approaches.title: Brute Force
approaches.intuition: Check all nodes to see if they satisfy the BST properties by comparing each node with all others.
approaches.algorithm: Perform a traversal of the tree and for each node, check if it is greater than all nodes in its left subtree and less than all nodes in its right subtree.

approaches.title: Optimal Approach
approaches.intuition: Use a recursive function to validate the BST properties by maintaining a range for each node, ensuring all values fall within the specified limits.
approaches.algorithm: Recursively check each node, updating the valid range for each node based on its parent nodes.

dry_run: Consider the input level-order: [2, 1, 3]. Start at the root (2). Check left child (1) is less than 2 and right child (3) is greater than 2. Both conditions are satisfied, hence it is a valid BST.
edge_cases: An empty tree should return true as it is a valid BST. A single node tree should also return true. Trees with duplicate values should return false. Large inputs should be handled efficiently without exceeding stack limits.
common_pitfalls: 1. Failing to account for the range of values correctly when traversing the tree. 2. Not handling duplicate values which invalidate the BST property. 3. Incorrectly assuming that any binary tree is a BST without checking the properties.
